hook.text: Shell Oil survived the 1973 oil crisis because they had already imagined it — through scenario planning.

buildup.text: Scenario planning doesn't predict the future. It prepares for multiple plausible futures.

discovery.text: Teams create three to four scenarios: best case, worst case, and two surprising but possible alternatives.

twist.text: The value isn't the scenarios themselves — it's the thinking process that stretches mental models.

climax.text: Projects that use scenario planning are more resilient because they've rehearsed for multiple realities.

punchline.text: You can't predict the future. You can prepare for it.

quiz.question: What is the primary benefit of scenario planning?
options: It prepares teams for multiple plausible futures, increasing resilience
It accurately predicts what will happen
